173

Hazrat Hud's (AH) name mentioned in the Holy Quran ____ times.

Correct Answer: 7
Show explanation
Explanation

List of Prophet Names mentiond in Quran

Adam 25 times
Harun 20 times
Sulayman 17 times
Ishaq 17 times
Dawud (David) 16 times
Yaqub (Jacob) 16 times
Ismael (Ishmael) 12 times
Shuayb 11 times
Salih 9 times
Zakaria 7 times
Hud 7 times
Yahya 5 times
Muhammad 4 times
174

Who was the first person to offer Namaz amongst the Ummah of the Prophet (SA W).

Correct Answer: Khadija (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

When Muhammad reported his first revelation from the Angel Gabriel (Jibril), Khadijah was the first person to accept Al-Haqq The Truth i.e. she accepted Islam.
175

The first step before distribution of the estate of a deceased Mohammedan is:

Correct Answer: Both a & b.
Show explanation
Explanation

After funeral expenses and payment of debt  estate can be distributed among legal heirs 

176

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, female sharers are:

Correct Answer: 8
Show explanation
Explanation

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, male sharers are Four.


177

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, male sharers are:

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, female sharers are Eight

178

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, sharers total is number are:

Correct Answer: 12
Show explanation
Explanation

According to Hanafi Law of Inheritance, classes of legal heirs are:

(a) Sharers.

(b) Residuaries.

(c) Distant Kindred.


179

Which of these properties is not heritable

Correct Answer: Self acquired
Show explanation
Explanation

Self acquired property is the property that you have purchased from your own income.

180

Talab-e-Mowasibat is made _______.

Correct Answer: Before decree
Show explanation
Explanation

Talab-e-Mowasibat is made Before decree

فرمان موصیبت فرمان سے پہلے بنائی گئی ہے۔

181

A marriage with a woman before completion of her iddat is?

Correct Answer: Irregular
Show explanation
Explanation

A marriage with a woman before completion of her iddat is Irregular 

عورت کے ساتھ عدت پوری ہونے سے پہلے شادی غیر قانونی ہے۔

Women iddat is 4 months & 10 days

182

Legitimacy may be presumed from ______ :

Correct Answer: Presumptive marriage
Show explanation
Explanation

 Legitimacy may be presumed from  Presumptive marriage 

قیاس آرائی کی شادی سے قانونی حیثیت کو سمجھا جا سکتا ہے۔

183

حضرت اسرافیل کا کیا کام ہے؟

Correct Answer: صور پھوکنا
Show explanation
Explanation

بڑے فرشتوں میں سے ایک فرشتہ مسلمانوں کے عقیدے کے مطابق خدا نے اسرافیل کو صور پھونکنے پر مامور کیا ہے۔ قرب قیامت کے وقت یہ خدا کے حکم سے صور پھونکے گے
184

Jehad is a word of which language?

Correct Answer: Arabic
Show explanation
Explanation
  • To Strive Hard is the literal meaning of Jihad
  • ***
  • Jihad becomes first in.2AH
  • 41 times the word jihad appears in Quran
  • Jihad is an Arabic word derived from Jahada. 
185

خلیفتہ الارض کس پیغمبر کا لقب ہے؟

Correct Answer: حضرت داؤد
Show explanation
Explanation

Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

The holy book Zaboor revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times.



186

ذولنون کس پیغمبر کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

Correct Answer: حضرت یونس
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Younis is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran with 109 verses (ayat).

Hazrat Yunus is named after the prophet Yunus (Jonah).

Hazrat Younis remained in the abdoman of fish for 40 days.

Hazrat Yunis is mentioned in the holy Quran: 4 times

Hazrat Younis (AS) is called Dhun-Nun 

 

187

Hazrat Yunas (علیہ) is addressed with two names one is ‘Dhun-Nun’ its meaning is_______?

Correct Answer: Lord Of the Fish
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Younis is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran with 109 verses (ayat).

Hazrat Yunus is named after the prophet Yunus (Jonah).

Hazrat Younis remained in the abdomen of fish for 40 days.

Hazrat Yunus is mentioned in the holy Quran: 4 times

Hazrat Younis (AS) is called Dhun-Nun 

 

188

خطیب الانبیاء کس کا لقب تھاـــــــــــــــ؟

Correct Answer: حضر شعیب علیہ السلام
Show explanation
Explanation

حضرت شعیب  مدین میں پیدا ہوئے۔

حضرت سعیب کا ذکر قرآن مجید میں دس سے بھی ذائد مرتبہ آیا ہے۔


Know about Hazrat Shoaib (AS)

Born: Midian 

Grave Location: Jordan

Father-in-law of Hazrat Moosa (AS)

Title: Speaker of Prophets 


189

آدم ثانی کس نبی کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

Correct Answer: حضرت نوح
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Nooh prophet was declared Adam 2ND

Because after his storm he followers died and the humanity will restarted as after adam .


storm طوفان

190

ابو البشر اور صفی اللہ کس پیغمبر کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

Correct Answer: حضرت آدم
Show explanation
Explanation

Aadam is believed to have been the first human being on Earth and first prophet of Islam. Adam's role as the father of the human race is looked upon by Muslims with reverence. Muslims also refer to his wife, Hawwa, as the "mother of mankind".


191

Habibullah is the title of which prophet?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)
Show explanation
Explanation

Titles of Prophets

Habibullah: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

Rooh Ullah: Hazrat Musa A.S

Speaker of Prophets: Hazrat Shoaib (AS)

Israel: Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) 

Rooh Ullah: Isa ibn Maryam 

192

ابلیس کا اصل نام کیا تھا؟

Correct Answer: ازازیل
Show explanation
Explanation

Azazil is usually seen as the original name of Iblis

The word 'Iblis' (إِبْلِيس) comes from the word Balasa (بَلَسَ), which means 'to despair'

Azazil is the name of Satan because he despairs from all goodness

liblis is the leader of the devils (shayāṭīn) in Islam.

 Iblis was thrown out of heaven after he refused to prostrate himself before Adam

193

A minor of sound mind is capable of disposing of his property by will to the extent of :

Correct Answer: None
Show explanation
Explanation

Minor is not capable of disposing his property .

194

With the fall of the Ottoman Empire, which of the following countries moved towards embracing secular nationalism ______?

Correct Answer: Turkey
Show explanation
Explanation

The Ottoman Empire was founded at the end of the 13th century in northwestern Anatolia in the town of Söğüt by the Turkoman tribal leader Osman I.

____________________________

Ankara is the capital while Istanbul is the largest city of Turkey.

Lira is the currency of Turkey.

195

According to Hazrat Abdullah Bin Abbas the last was decended on Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), how many days before death?

Correct Answer: 7 or 9
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad's first revelation was an event described in Islamic tradition as taking place in 610 AD.

5 ayats

Surah Alq

At the age of 40 in cave Hira

196

Where did the Abbasid Caliphate reestablish the Caliphate in 1261 CE?

Correct Answer: Egypt
Show explanation
Explanation

In 1261, following the devastation of Baghdad by the Mongols, the Mamluk rulers of Egypt re-established the Abbasid caliphate in Cairo.

________________________________

Abbasid caliphate, second of the two great dynasties of the Muslim empire of the caliphate.

It overthrew the Umayyad caliphate in 750 ce and reigned as the Abbasid caliphate until it was destroyed by the Mongol invasion in 1258.


197

The Caliph of the Abbasid Caliphate was elected from a number of religious wise men.

Correct Answer: FALSE
Show explanation
Explanation

The Abbasid caliphs established the city of Baghdad in 762 CE. It became a center of learning and the hub of what is known as the Golden Age of Islam.

_________________________-

Abbasid caliphate, second of the two great dynasties of the Muslim empire of the caliphate.

It overthrew the Umayyad caliphate in 750 ce and reigned as the Abbasid caliphate until it was destroyed by the Mongol invasion in 1258.

198

What area of culture flourished during the Golden Age of Islam?

Correct Answer: All
Show explanation
Explanation

The Islamic Golden Age, traditionally dated from the 8th century to the 13th century, during which much of the historically Islamic world was ruled by various caliphates and science, economic development, and cultural works flourished.

199

What region of the world did the Umayyads continue to rule well after the fall of the Umayyad Caliphate?

Correct Answer: Iberian Peninsula
Show explanation
Explanation

The Umayyads incorporated the Caucasus, Transoxiana, Sindh, the Maghreb, and the Iberian Peninsula  into the Muslim world.

__________________

Ameer Muawiyah was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate, serving from 661 until his death. (30 years)

200

What was the official language of the Umayyad Caliphate?

Correct Answer: Arabic
Show explanation
Explanation

The Umayyad Caliphate  was the second of the four major caliphates established after the death of Muhammad.

The caliphate was ruled by the Umayyad dynasty  "Sons of Umayyah".

The third caliph of the Rashidun Caliphate, Uthman ibn Affan was also a member of the Umayyad clan. 

201

Which of the following regions was NOT a part of the Umayyad Caliphate at its peak?

Correct Answer: South America
Show explanation
Explanation

The Umayyad Caliphate controlled the Middle East, parts of India, much of North Africa, and Spain.

The region of Syria remained the Umayyads' main power base thereafter, and Damascus was their capital.

202

What were non-Muslims required to do under the Umayyad Caliphate?

Correct Answer: Pay a special tax
Show explanation
Explanation

 Umayyad dynasty non-Muslim population had autonomy, their judicial matters were dealt with in accordance with their own laws and by their own religious heads or their appointees.

Non-Muslims paid a poll tax for policing to the central state.

203

Azra is a title of____?

Correct Answer: Bibi Maryam
Show explanation
Explanation

Maryam daughter of Imram, mother of Isa , holds a singularly exalted place in Islam as the only woman named in the Quran.

204

Bibi Safoora was daughter of prophet?

Correct Answer: Shoaib
Show explanation
Explanation

Shuaib,  was an ancient Midianite Nabi in Islam.

He is sometimes identified with the Biblical Jethro.

He is mentioned in the Quran a total of 11 times.

________________

Zipporah  is mentioned in the Book of Exodus as the wife of Moses

205

A person who has an intention to offer only Hajj at Meeqat is called_______?

Correct Answer: Mufrid
Show explanation
Explanation

A Mufrid ( مفرد) is a Hajj pilgrim who is performing Hajj al-Ifrad, which involves making one Niyyah for the sole purpose of performing Hajj without Umrah.
206

The real name of Umm-ul-Momineen “Bibi Ume Salma” was___________?

Correct Answer: Hind
Show explanation
Explanation

Hind al-Makhzumiyah was one of the wives of Islamic prophet Muhammad.

"Umm Salama" was her kunya meaning, "mother of Salama".

207

Jannatul Mu'alla is a ________?

Correct Answer: Cemetery
Show explanation
Explanation

Jannat al-Mu'alla also known as the "Cemetery of Ma'la is a cemetery to the north of Al-Masjid Al-Haram, and near the Mosque of the Jinn in Makkah, Saudi Arabia. It is the place where the Islamic Prophet Muhammad's wife, grandfather, and other ancestors are buried.
208

Which letter has mostly used after alif (الف) in the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Noon
Show explanation
Explanation

The word Alif used in Quran 43542 times

The word Noon used in Quran 7270 Times

209

Where is the grave of Hazrat Yaqub (AS)?

Correct Answer: Palestine
Show explanation
Explanation

Muslims hold that Jacob was the son of Isaac and that he preached the Oneness of God throughout his life.
210

______________will be first person questioned on Day of Qiyamat.

Correct Answer: Jibraeel (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation

In Islamic eschatology, the Day of Resurrection is believed to be God's final assessment of humanity.

Other names for it in the Quran are the Day of Reckoning, the Last Day, and the Hour.

6th article of Faith

211

حدیث کے مطابق جو ہر فرض نماز کے بعد پڑھے گا۔ اسے جنت سے کوئی چیز نہیں روکنے والی نہیں ہے۔ سوائے موت کے۔

Correct Answer: آیتہ الکرسی
Show explanation
Explanation

آیت الکرسی سورہ بقرہ کا حصہ ہے

212

آیتالکرسی کس سورت کا حصہ ہے۔

Correct Answer: Surah Al-Baqra
Show explanation
Explanation

The Throne Verse is the 255th verse of the 2nd surah of the Quran, Al-Baqarah. The verse speaks about how nothing and nobody is regarded to be comparable to Allah. This is one of the best-known verses of the Quran and is widely memorised and displayed in the Muslim world. It is often recited to ward off evil spirits.

213

سورة البقرہ__________ صورت ہے

Correct Answer: مدنی
Show explanation
Explanation


سورة البقرہمدنی صورت ہے

related question 

AL-BAQARAH (the Cow) has been so named from the story of the Cow occurring in this Surah (vv. 67-73).

Al-Baqarah is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.

It consists of 286 verses

282 verse in chapter Al-Baqara. this verse is the longest verse in the longest chapter of the Quran

The 282 verse of Surah Al Baqara is known as 'āyatu d-dayn) 

Position: Juzʼ 1–3

Classification: Medinan

No. of Rukus: 40

 25,500 letters

13 Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ) is mentioned in Sura Baqarah

183 Ayat of Surah Al-Baqarah describes the obligation of soam




214

سورہ بقرہ میں کل آیات ـــــــــــــــــــ ہیں؟

Correct Answer: 286
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Baqarah  meaning: "The Heifer" or "The Cow"

 Al-Baqarah is the second and longest surah of the Quran.

Al-Baqarah  consists of 286 ayat, 6,201 words and 25,500 letters.




215

آخرت کے لغوی معنی ہے؟

Correct Answer: بعد میں آنے والی چیز
Show explanation
Explanation

قرآن مجید میں آخرت کیلیئے یوم الحاقتہ، یوم الحساب اور یوم الدین جیسے الفاظ استعمال ہوئے۔


216

جو شخص آپ ﷺ کے بعد رسالت یا نبوت کا دعوی کرے گا وہ جھوٹا اور دائرہ اسلام سے خارج ہے۔ اس کو عقیدہ__________ کہتے ہیں۔

Correct Answer: ختم نبوت
Show explanation
Explanation

جو شخص آپ ﷺ کے بعد رسالت یا نبوت کا دعوی کرے گا وہ جھوٹا اور دائرہ اسلام سے خارج ہے۔ اس کو عقیدہ ختم نبوت کہتے ہیں۔


217

رسالت کا آغاز کس پیغمبر سے ہوا؟

Correct Answer: حضرت آدم
Show explanation
Explanation

رسالت کے معنی ہیں پیغام پہنچانا اور رسول کے معنی ہیں پیغام پہنچانے والے۔ 

رسالت کا آغاز حضرت آدم سے ہوا اور آخری نبی حضرت محمد پر ختم ہوا۔




218

نبی اکرم ﷺ نے فرمایا میں آخری نبی ہوں اور تم آخری ۔۔۔۔ ہو۔

Correct Answer: امت
Show explanation
Explanation

نبی اکرم ﷺ نے فرمایا میں آخری نبی ہوں اور تم آخری امت ہو۔


***********


219

زکوۃ کب فرض ہوا؟

Correct Answer: دو ہجری
Show explanation
Explanation

Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

Zakat literally means Purification.

When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H (properly implemented in 8 Hijri.

The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat

One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir


“Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)

****

612.36 grams of silver is the Ratio of Zakat in grams of Silver
220

ــــــــــــــ کا دوسرا نام صدقہ بھی ہے۔

Correct Answer: بھلائی
Show explanation
Explanation

بھلائی کے معنی ہیں نیکی، اچھا سلوک یا برتاو

221

شب قدر کتنے مہینوں سے افضل ہے ؟

Correct Answer: 1000
Show explanation
Explanation

قرآن مجید میں شب قدر کی عبادت کو ہزار مہینوں سے بہتر قرار دیا گیا ہے۔ اس حساب سے اس رات کی عبادت 83 سال اور 4 مہینے بنتی ہے۔

222

What Caliphate followed the Umayyad Caliphate?

Correct Answer: Abbasid Caliphate
Show explanation
Explanation

The Abbasids overthrew the Umayyad dynasty in 750 CE, supporting the mawali, or non-Arab Muslims, by moving the capital to Baghdad in 762 CE.

________

Ameer Muawiyah was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate, serving from 661 until his death. (30 years)

223

The idol Manaat was fixed between Makkah and Madina. who worshipped it?

Correct Answer: Banu Aos and Banu Khazraj
Show explanation
Explanation

Aws and Khazraj were two Arab tribes of Medina during the time of the Prophet Muhammad. Later the conflict between these two tribes, Banu Aws and Banu Khazraj, died out and they jointly became known as "ansar" or helper.
224

Died on the tenth of Ramadan 10th Nabvi?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

"Hazrat Khadija's"  important Questions and information

Hazrat Khadija (RA) was the ________ wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

First

*

Hazart Khadija (R.A) Born:    

AD 555 Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia present-day Saudi Arabia

*

Hazart Khadija (R.A) Died:

10 Ramadan BH 3  c. 619  Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia

*

Hazart Khadija (R.A) Resting place  

Jannat al-Mu'alla, Mecca

Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) passed away when she was ______ and Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was ____ years old.

65 – 50

At the time of the marriage, Hazrat Khadijah was 40 years old. Muhammad was 25 years.

All of the prophet Muhammad ﷺ is children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim

*

Hazrat Khadija (RA) is commonly regarded by Muslims as the _______.

Mother of the Muslims

*

Friend of Khadija (رضہ) ____ carried message of Nikah?

Nafeesa

*

Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) belonged to the tribe of ___?

Banu Asad

*

***

Hazrat Khadija's servant, Maysra, who accompanied the Holy Prophet on his journey was full of praise for him.

Meysara, Lady Khadijah's (s.a) special servant.

NS16-3-2023

225

SIn salat, saying Darood is____?

Correct Answer: Sunnah
Show explanation
Explanation

Saying Darood in Salat is considered Sunnah

Because It was regularly practiced by the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

Salat became Farz on the 10th nabvi.
226

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of_________ to Abu Zar Ghaffari R.A ?

Correct Answer: Sheikh-ul-Islam RA
Show explanation
Explanation

Abu Dharr Al-Ghifari Al-Kinani  was the fourth or fifth person converting to Islam, and from the Muhajirun.

227

The “Ridda Wars” were fought by which caliph?

Correct Answer: 1st caliph
Show explanation
Explanation

The Ridda Wars  or Apostasy Wars, were a series of military campaigns launched by the Caliph Abu Bakr against rebel Arabian tribes during 632 and 633, just after the death of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad.

228

The Battle of Camel was fought between Hazrat Ali (رضہ) and ______?

Correct Answer: Bibi Aisha
Show explanation
Explanation


The battle was fought between the army of the fourth caliph Ali, on one side, and the rebel army led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr, on the other side.  

The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra,

took place at Basra, Iraq on 7 November 656.

Strength
~10,000~10,000
Casualties and losses
>400-500>2,500
229

How many years Aisha(RA) remained alive after the death of Muhammad ﷺ?

Correct Answer: 44
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Aisha (RA) lived 45 years after the death of Prophet Muhammad (SAW)

She was 18 years old when Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) died

Aisha died at her home in Medina on 17 Ramadan 58 AH (16 July 678).

She was 67 years old.

Abu Hurairah led her funeral prayer after the tahajjud (night) prayer

Aisha was buried at Jannat al-Baqi.


Additional Information

Hazrat Ayesha was the 3rd wife and most learned of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths

Muhammad married Hazrat Ayesha when he was 53 years old and she was only six years old.

He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.

Hazrat Ayesha was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker 

She gives the title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man




230

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of_________ to Hazrat Usama bin Zaid R.A ?

Correct Answer: Hub Rasool-Ullah
Show explanation
Explanation

Usama ibn Zayd  companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

He was the son of Zayd ibn Harithah, Muhammad's freed slave and adopted son, and Umm Ayman .

Died: .680  Medina, Arabia

231

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of_________ to Hazrat Owais Qarni R.A ?

Correct Answer: Khair-ut-Tabe’en
Show explanation
Explanation

Awais Karni was born in Asir region of Saudi Arabia in 594CE.

He was 28 years of age when Holy Prophet (ﷺ) had migrated to Madinah۔

232

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of_________ to Hazrat Amr bin al-Aas R.A ?

Correct Answer: Sayyed-ul-Muslimeen
Show explanation
Explanation

Amr ibn al-As al-Sahmi was the Arab commander who led the Muslim conquest of Egypt and served as its governor in 640–646 and 658–664. 

233

The third daughter of our Holy prophet ﷺ was ___________.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umme Kulsom (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Umm-e-Kalthum was 3rd daughter and 4th child of the Holy Prophet ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

After the death of her sister Ruqayya left Uthman a widower, he married Umm Kulthum

Umm Kulthum died in 630 (8 AH) at age of 27

She was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


Additional Information:


Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

234

In the Period Hazrat Umar most battle Muslim fought against?

Correct Answer: Persians
Show explanation
Explanation

1.      Conquest of (Roman Empire) Levant (634–638)

                          I.                  Muslim conquest of Syria

                        II.                  Muslim conquest of Armenia

2.      Conquest of (Roman Empire)  Egypt (640–642)

3.      Conquest of mainland Persia (642-644)

235

From where the word Hadis drived?

Correct Answer: Tahdis
Show explanation
Explanation
236

What is the meaning of Tahdis

Correct Answer: To inform
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hadith is an Arabic word, which literally means statement, talk, story, conversation or communication.

237

What is the Duration of Madni Period?

Correct Answer: 9 Years, 9 Months, 9 days,
Show explanation
Explanation

Duration of Madni Period is 9 Years, 9 Months, 9 days.

Characteristics of Medinan surahs:

1.      Details of Islamic jurisprudence and legal system (Laws)

2.      Long verses

3.      Easy vocabulary
238

Makkhi Surahs were revealed in how many years?

Correct Answer: 13
Show explanation
Explanation

Makkhi Surahs were revealed in 13 Years

Madni Surahs were revealed in 10 Years
239

What an authentic Hadis is called___________?

Correct Answer: Sahih
Show explanation
Explanation

In its classic form a hadith has two parts:

the chain of narrators who have transmitted the report (the isnad), and the main text of the report (the matn).
240

Who solicited Hazrat Usman (رضہ) to send the copies of Quran to different countries?

Correct Answer: Hazrat hadifa-bin-Yamaan
Show explanation
Explanation

Solicited درخواست کی

Hazrat Umar proposed the compilation of Holy Quran.
Hazart Zaid bin sabit ( R.A) firstly arranged and wrote the Quran on paper in book form.
241

The part of prayer the Holy Prophet did is called

Correct Answer: Sunnat
Show explanation
Explanation

There are three types of Sunnah.

The first is the sayings of the prophet – Sunnah Qawliyyah/Hadith.

The second is Sunnah Al Filiyya.

Third Sunnah Taqririyyah

242

Who was sent by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) as his envoy to Quraish of Makkah on the eve of Sulah-e- Hudaibiya?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

 Mentioned in Surah Fatah

It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammad PBUH, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca in January 628 (6 AH). It helped to decrease tension between Makkah and Madina, authorized Muhammad's followers to return the following year in a peaceful pilgrimage, later known as The First Pilgrimage.

243

The explanation of Salat is in the_______

Correct Answer: AHAADITH
Show explanation
Explanation

Salat, the daily ritual prayer enjoined upon all Muslims as one of the five Pillars of Islam.

Ṣalāh  is an Arabic word that means to pray or bless.

244

Name of the first Caliph of Islam who introduced the Jail Department?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Umar (R.A) was the first Caliph of Islam who introduced the Jail Department

حضرت عمر رضی اللہ عنہ اسلام کے پہلے خلیفہ تھے جنہوں نے محکمہ جیل کو متعارف کرایا

245

The Wahi was sent in the month of __?.

Correct Answer: Ramzan
Show explanation
Explanation

Prophet Muhammad first received revelations in 609 CE in a cave on Mount Hira
246

What does the breath of fasting person smell like according to Hadith?

Correct Answer: Musk
Show explanation
Explanation

The smell that issues from the mouth of the fasting person is more pleasant to Allah than the smell of musk.

247

The kind of Hadith which doesn't reach to Twatur is called___

Correct Answer: Khabari Wahid
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Khabar al-Wāḥid  a narration of hadiths which is not regarded al-mutawatir hadith which means the number of narrators in all generations of transmission is not enough to ascertain the authenticity of Khabar.

248

_____,was the brother of Hazrat Ali (R.A) who was taken as prisoner in the battle of Badr?

Correct Answer: Aqeel
Show explanation
Explanation

Aqeel ibn Abi Talib was a companion and first cousin of the Islamic prophet Muhammad. He was known by the kunya Abu Yazid. He is also said to be the common ancestor of the Somali clans such as the Darod clan.
249

Book named “The Voice of Human Justice ” is the biography of______?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

The Voice of Human Justice is an English translation of Sautu'l 'Adālati'l Insaniyah, a book written in Arabic by George Jordac, a Christian author from Lebanon. The book is a biography of Ali ibn Abi Talib. The contents of the book were drawn from the Nahj al-Balagha of Ali. The book depicts the personality of Ali.
250

“Nahjul Balagha” is authored by

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

He was the fourth caliph of Islam.

He accepted Islam at the age of 10.

251

Hazrat Abu Bakr sent which companion to Syria?

Correct Answer: Khalid bin Waleed
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Abu Bakar was first khalifa of Islam


252

Al-Ateeq (The Delivered One) is the title of:

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddique (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Ateeq (The Delivered One) The Holy prophet (PBUH)  said to Abu Bakr, “You are the delivered one (Ateeq) of Allah from the Fire” so, he was named “Ateeq”, and in the narration of Aai’shah who said: Abu Bakr entered upon the Messenger of Allaah (ﷺ)who said to him, “Glad tidings for you are the Ateeq of Allah from the Fire.” [Al-Tirmidhi, Silsilah al-Sahihah, Saheeh.]

253

The Battle of Camel was fought at _______?

Correct Answer: Basra
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra, took place at Basra, Iraq on 7 November 656.

It was first war during Imam Ali's (a) caliphate, took place between Nakithun (Oath-breakers)

254

Where tomb of Bibi Pak Daman is located?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Bibi Pak Daman is the mausoleum of Ruqayyah bint Ali located in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

255

Qaim-ul-Lail and Saim-ul-Nahar are the titles of _____?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Ali ibn Abi Talib was the fourth caliph from 656 until his assassination in 661.
Ali ibn Abi Talib Born: March 17, 599 AD, Kaaba, Mecca, Saudi Arabia
Ali ibn Abi Talib Place of burial: Imam Ali Holy Shrine, Najaf, Iraq
256

The most exemplary administration out of the rightly guided caliphs was that of_____?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

He was an expert Muslim jurist known for his pious and just nature, which earned him the epithet Al-Farooq

Hazrat Umar Reign: 23 August 634 CE – 3 November 644 CE
Hazrat Umar Tribe: Quraysh (‎Banu Adi‎)‎
Hazrat Umar Father: Khattab ibn Nufayl
Hazrat Umar Died: November 644 (aged 59–60)

257

______ is the best drink mentioned in Holy Qur’an.

Correct Answer: Milk
Show explanation
Explanation

It is a verse from the Quran, specifically Surah An-Nahl (The Bee), verse 66 (16:66).

We give you drink from what is in their bellies – between excretion and blood – pure milk, palatable to drinkers.

258

Bait e Rizwan, also known as Bait e Shajrah, took place in 6th AH under the _________ tree?

Correct Answer: Keekar
Show explanation
Explanation

Bait-e-Rizwan took place in 6th Hijrah.

Jewish tribe of Banu Nuzair expelled from Madina in 4th H.

Bait-e-Rizwan is also known as Bait-e-Shajra made under Keekar tree.

259

Shaddad was famous king of Bani Aad; this nation resided in Yemen and ____________ was sent to this nation?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Hood (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Shaddād  was believed to be the king of the lost Arabian city of Iram of the Pillars, an account of which is mentioned in Sura 89 of the Qur'an.

Various sources suggest Shaddad was the son of 'Ad al-Miltat ibn Saksak ibn Wa'il ibn Himyar

260

The name of the horse of Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed was___________?

Correct Answer: Ashjar
Show explanation
Explanation

They narrate that horse of Khalid bin Walid, named Ashqar was whipping on the grave of a great martyr and the companions were amazed at this expression of grief of this animal.

Khalid Ibn Al-Walid died in 642 was buried in Homs, Syria.

Place of birth: Mecca


261

Which prophet of Allah loved horses?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Suleiman A.S
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Suliman (علیہ) Born: Jerusalem, Shaam
Hazrat Suliman (علیہ) Parent(s): Dawud (father)
Hazrat Suliman (علیہ) Title: King of Israel

262

“Arbaeen” is the book of Hadith in which there are:__________?

Correct Answer: 40 Ahadith
Show explanation
Explanation

Arbaʽein  is a Shiite religious observance that occurs forty days after the Day of Ashura.

263

The biggest hypocrite was in Madina?

Correct Answer: Abdullah Bin Abi
Show explanation
Explanation

Abd-Allah ibn Ubayy, also called Ibn Salul in reference to his grandfather, was a chief of the Arab tribe Banu Khazraj and one of the leading men of Medina.

Upon the arrival of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, Ibn Ubayy seemingly became a Muslim, but remained treacherous to Islam until his death. 

264

When Gambling was banned?

Correct Answer: 4 A.H
Show explanation
Explanation

Gambling is the wagering something of value on an event with an uncertain outcome with the intent of winning something else of value.

265

Along with Abdullah, which foster sister(s) of Muhammad (PBUH) embraced Islam?

Correct Answer: Both of these
Show explanation
Explanation

Gambling  is the wagering something of value ("the stakes") on an event with an uncertain outcome with the intent of winning something else of value.

266

Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) spent his last days in the cottage of_________?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ayesha (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hazrat Ayesha (RA) Muhammad's third and youngest wife

Title "Mother of the Believers", referring to the description of Muhammad's wives in the Qur'an.

267

Hazrat Ibraheem (AS) was sent to ________ after leaving Hazrat Hajirah and Hazrat Ismaeel in But’ha valley?

Correct Answer: Jordan
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammad Name mentioned in Holy Quran in 4 time.

Hazrat Ibrahim (AH) name mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

268

Hazrat Yahya (AS) was sent to the people of_________?

Correct Answer: Jordan
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Yahya buried ant The Umayyad Mosque, Damascus, Syria

269

Where is the grave of Hazrat Saleh (A.S)?

Correct Answer: Lebanon
Show explanation
Explanation

"Hazrat Suleh (A.S) was chosen for prophethood at the age of 16,

270

Hazrat Ilyas (AS) prayed for his nation; and it rained after ________ years.

Correct Answer: 312
Show explanation
Explanation

Ilyās  was a prophet  who was sent to guide the Children of Israel

He was given the prophetic mission to prevent people from worshipping idols.

Ilyas is the prophetic predecessor to Alyasa.

271

The grave of prophet (PBUH) father was in _______?

Correct Answer: Madina
Show explanation
Explanation

Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib was the father of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

He was the son of Abdul-Muttalib ibn Hashim and Fatimah bint Amr of the Makhzum clan.

He was married to Āminah bint Wahb. 

Muhammad was their only offspring.

272

Abbreviated Letters Alif Laam Meem, Ya Seen, Ha Meem, etc. are known as the Huroof_________?

Correct Answer: Al-Muqattaat
Show explanation
Explanation

The mysterious letters  "disjoined letters" or "disconnected letters" are combinations of between one and five Arabic letters figuring at the beginning of 29 out of the 114 chapters (surahs) of the Quran just after the Bismillāh Islamic phrase.

273

Period between Hazrat Essa to Birth of Prophet Mohammad Saw is called?

Correct Answer: Period of Fitrah
Show explanation
Explanation

Fitra or fitrah is the state of purity and innocence Muslims believe all humans to be born ... of the concept of Primordial Nature is mentioned 14 other times.

274

Who used to write down every word of the Holy Prophet SAW speech?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah Bin Umer R.A
Show explanation
Explanation

Abdullah ibn Omar ibn al-Khattab was companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and son of the second Caliph Omar.

He was a prominent authority in hadith and law.

275

Where is Jabl-e-Zaintoon, Mount of olive?

Correct Answer: Palestine
Show explanation
Explanation

 The mount has been used as a Jewish cemetery for over 3,000 years and holds approximately 150,000 graves, making it central in the tradition of Jewish cemeteries.

276

How many times, the word ‘Allah’, is repeated in the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: 2698
Show explanation
Explanation

The name of God (Allah) is written 2,698 or 2699 times repeated in the Quran.
277

Politus, on the directions of the Roman Governor, ordered the execution of ___________?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Essa (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Predecessor Yahya (John the Baptist)

Successor Muhammad

Īsā ibn Maryam is the penultimate prophet and messenger of God.


278

In Holy Quran, the Word “Azeez” is used for Quran. What does it mean?

Correct Answer: Dominator
Show explanation
Explanation

 Aziz in Arabic is derived from the root  with a meaning of "strong, powerful" and the adjective has acquired its meaning of "dear, darling, precious".

279

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) first openly preached Islam on ___________; and addressed after the conquest of Makkah on the same place.

Correct Answer: Jabl-e-Safa
Show explanation
Explanation

Born  570 CE in Mecca, Muhammad was raised by his uncle Abu Talib and later worked as a merchant.

280

Allah will reward Jannat for _ deeds.

Correct Answer: Good
Show explanation
Explanation

 Allah will reward Jannat for good deeds

 اللہ تعالیٰ نیک اعمال کا بدلہ جنت میں دے گا۔

ND 30-03-2923



281

Which of the following has been stressed to be done before every namaz (prayer)?

Correct Answer: Use Miswak
Show explanation
Explanation

Using Miswak has been stressed to be done before every namaz (prayer).Prayer (Namaz) requires proper cleanliness.

Every Muslim is obligated to pray five times a day

Fajr (prayed at dawn)

Dhuhr (prayed at midday)

Asr (prayed in the afternoon)

Maghrib (prayed at sunset)

Isha (prayed at night)


ND 30-03-2923

282

Which of the following worships requires proper cleanliness?

Correct Answer: Prayer
Show explanation
Explanation

Prayer (Namaz) requires proper cleanliness.

Every Muslim is obligated to pray five times a day

Fajr (prayed at dawn)

Dhuhr (prayed at midday)

Asr (prayed in the afternoon)

Maghrib (prayed at sunset)

Isha (prayed at night)

***

***

ND 30-03-2923




283

​ Belief in the oneness of Allah and expression of it through the tongue is called:

Correct Answer: Tauheed
Show explanation
Explanation

Tauheed ​واحد ماننا

Tawhid means "unification of God in Islam (Allāh)"

Belief in the oneness of Allah and expression of it through the tongue is called Tauheed.

Shirk is the opposite of Tauheed.

***

ND 30-03-2923



 

284

What is the meaning of Ashra Mubashra?

Correct Answer: All of these above
Show explanation
Explanation

Ashra Mubashra were the close companions of the Holy Prophet who will be awarded Jannah


Following is the List with the Names of Ashra Mubashra.


1. Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A)

2. Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)

3. Hazrat Usman (R.A)

4. Hazrat Ali (R.A)

5. Hazrat Zubair ibn-e-Awam (R.A)

6. Hazrat Talha (R.A)

7. Hazrat Abdul Rehman bin-e-Auf (R.A)

8. Hazrat Abu Obaida bin-al-Jarah (R.A)

9. Hazrat Saad bin-e-Abi Waqas (R.A)

10. Hazrat Saeed bin-e-Zaid (R.A)

***

***

ND 30-03-2923

285

Mohammad saw is called “Farqaleet” in injeel. What does “Farqaleet means?

Correct Answer: One who gives comfort
Show explanation
Explanation

“Farqaleet means One who gives comfort 

286

What is the primary emphasis of the Qur’an upon?

Correct Answer: The Doctrine of Tauheed
Show explanation
Explanation

Tawhid,in Islam, the oneness of God, in the sense that he is one and there is no god but he, as stated in the shahādah  “There is no god but God and Muhammad is His prophet.” 

287

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of_________ to Naqeeb-e-Ansar ?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Talha Ansari RA
Show explanation
Explanation

Abū Ṭalḥa, was a renowned Companion of the Islamic prophet Muḥammad and one of the Anṣār of Medina.

Family Banu Khazraj, which after the Hijrah came to be known as the Ansar.

Born: 585 AD

288

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of _____________ to Hazrat Huzefa bin -al-Yaman R.A ?

Correct Answer: Mahram-e-Raza-e-Nabuwat
Show explanation
Explanation

Abu Hudhayfa ibn Utbah, full name Abu Hudhayfa ibn Utbah ibn Rabi'ah was an early companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

He was the son of Utbah ibn Rabi'ah, one of the Leaders of Banu Abd Shams.

He was the brother of Walid ibn Utbah and Hind bint Utbah, the wife of Abu Sufyan.

289

The city of FUSTAT was founded by ________ ?

Correct Answer: Amr bin Al-As
Show explanation
Explanation

Founded in 641 by the Muslim conqueror of Egypt.

On the east bank of the Nile River, south of modern Cairo, al-Fusṭāṭ was the earliest Arab settlement in Egypt and site of the province's first mosque, Jāmiʿ ʿAmr.

290

First translation Quran in any European language was made in Latin by Robert of Ketton in 1143 and it was printed in ___________.

Correct Answer: 1543
Show explanation
Explanation

 Ketton's translation remained the standard Latin translation for four centuries.

It appeared in print for the first time in 1542/43 as the first printed Latin translation of the Qur'an.

291

To which Prophet did Jews call the son of ALLAH?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Uzair A.S
Show explanation
Explanation

Uzair is a figure who is mentioned in the Quran, Surah At-Tawba,  which states that he was revered by the Jews as "the son of God".

292

The famous Personalities whom Accepted Islam after the Conquest of Makkah Were_______?

Correct Answer: All of these
Show explanation
Explanation

The conquest of Mecca  was the conquering of the town of Mecca by Muslims led by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in December 629 or January 630 AD (Julian), 10–20 Ramadan, 8 AH. The conquest marked the end of the wars between the followers of Muhammad and the Quraysh tribe.
293

The downfall of the Umayyads in Damascus happened in ______?

Correct Answer: 132 A.H
Show explanation
Explanation

The Umayyad dynasty lasted less than a century in Damascus before it was driven out in 750 by the Abbasid dynasty.

Umayyad Caliphate was the second Muslim Caliphate that emerged after Rashidun Caliphate.

294

What was the age of Hazrat Ammar ibn Yasir (رضہ) when he was martyred?

Correct Answer: 90
Show explanation
Explanation

Ammār ibn Yāsir, is considered to be one of the most loyal and beloved companions of Muhammad and ‘Ali and to Muslims.

Born: 570 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

Died: 657 AD, Syria

Cause of Death: Martyrdom in the Battle of Siffin, Al-Sham
295

When did the Shaikh al Islam Hazrat Khwaja Abdullah Ansari of Herat also known as Pir-i Herat was born ?

Correct Answer: 4 May 1006
Show explanation
Explanation

Shaikh al Islam Hazrat Khwaja Abdullah Ansari was Born 1006 Herat, Khorasan, Ghaznavid Empire

Died 1088 (aged 81-82) in Herat, Khorasan, Ghaznavid Empire.

296

The Muslim death toll on both sides in the Battle of Camel was about:_____?

Correct Answer: 10,000
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra, took place at Basra, Iraq on 7 November 656.

The Battle of the Camel fought between Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), and Hazrat A'isha (R.A)
297

By whom Prophet ﷺ learnt Salat & ablution?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Jibrail (علیہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Jibrail A.S was also the guide of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ .
298

House of Wisdom (Bayt-ul-Hikamah) was established by _________?

Correct Answer: Harun al-Rashid
Show explanation
Explanation

The Bayt al-Ḥikmah (House of Wisdom) founded in 830ad in Baghdad.

It was established by Harun Rasheed.

299

Bibi Balqees was the Ruler of___?

Correct Answer: Yemen
Show explanation
Explanation

Bilqis (Queen of Sheba), Prophet Sulayman (pbuh), and the Hud-Hud story mentioned in Surah Namal of Holy Quran.
300

What is name of 24th Para of the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Faman Azlam
Show explanation
Explanation

Quran Para 24 called Faman Azlam (فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ)
301

What is the Original Name of Muʿtaṣim bi’llāh?

Correct Answer: Abu Ishaq Muhammad ibn Harun al-Rashid
Show explanation
Explanation

Abū Isḥāq Muḥammad ibn Hārūn al-Rashīd better known by his regnal name al-Muʿtaṣim biʾllāh, was the eighth Abbasid caliph, ruling from 833 until his death in 842. A younger son of Caliph Harun al-Rashid, he rose to prominence through his formation of a private army composed predominantly of Turkic slave-soldiers.
302

In which month Allah forgives 10 lakh Muslims every day at the time of Aftari?

Correct Answer: Ramadan
Show explanation
Explanation

Ramadan, is the ninth month of the Islamic calendar.
303

Which prophet mentioned first in the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

The first prophet was Adam, who was also the first man, created by Allah in his image.

304

Which Surah of the Holy Quran deals with the laws of marriage?

Correct Answer: Nisa
Show explanation
Explanation

An-Nisa is the fourth chapter (surah) of the Quran, with 176 verses (ayat).
No. of verses: 176
No. of Rukus: 24
305

Which Sura is the name of one Holy war?

Correct Answer: Ahzab
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of the Trench  romanized Ghazwat al-Khandaq also known as the Battle of Khandaq  and the Battle of the Confederates Ghazwat al-Ahzab), was a 27-day-long defence by Muslims of Yathrib (now Medina) from Arab and Jewish tribes. The strength of the confederate armies is estimated around 10,000 men with six hundred horses and some camels, while the Medinan defenders numbered 3,000.
306

The tenth wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ was _______.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Memona (رضہ)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Memona was the tenth wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

She was 36 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 60 years ago

 She lived with Muhammadﷺ for three years until his death

She was the daughter of Hazrat Harith (RA)

She was first married to Abu Ruhm ibn Abd al-Uzza. 



Additional Information:

Nabi e Akram (ﷺ) had eleven (11) wives also known as “Azwaaj e Mutahharaat”
307

Who was the third son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim
Show explanation
Explanation

Ibrahim was the third son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Born in Dhu al-Hijjah 8 AH 

He has died at the age of 2 on Shawwal 29, 10 AH

He was buried in Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

Only 1 son (child) was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA) 


Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

308

Hazrat Ibrahim son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ is burried in _________.

Correct Answer: Jannat ul Baki
Show explanation
Explanation

Ibrahim ibn Muhammad  was buried in Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

Ibrahim was the third (youngest) son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Born in Dhu al-Hijjah 8 AH 
He has died at the age of 2 on Shawwal 29, 10 AH
 
 

Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 
 
All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).
309

In which Surah of the Holy Quran the name of Allah repeated five times?

Correct Answer: Haj
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Ḥajj is the 22nd chapter (sūrah) of the Quran with 78 verses.

This surah takes its name from the 27th verse.
310

In which Surah of the Holy Quran the backbiter condemned?

Correct Answer: Humaza
Show explanation
Explanation

In Surah Al-Hujurat-Aya 12


اے لوگو جو ایمان لائے ہو، بہت گمانوں سے بچو، بے شک بعض گمان گناہ ہوتے ہیں، اور ایک دوسرے کی جاسوسی نہ کرو، نہ غیبت کرو، کیا تم میں سے کوئی یہ پسند کرے گا کہ جب مر جائے تو اپنے بھائی کا گوشت کھائے

Al-Humazah is also known as "The Backbiter", "The Slanderer"

It is the 104th chapter of the Quran,

It consists of  9 verses.

311

The most disliked thing by Allah Ta’ala is ____?

Correct Answer: Divorce
Show explanation
Explanation
 The term Talaq is commonly translated as "repudiation" or simply "divorce".
312

In Surah ________, every verse ends with letter ‘Ra’. 2

Correct Answer: Kausar
Show explanation
Explanation

The Surah Al Kawthar is the shortest surah of Holy Quran.
The Surah Baqarah is longest surah of Holy Quran.

313

The first daughter of our Holy prophet ﷺ was _______.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Zainab (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
Hazrat Zainab was the first and youngest daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah
She married her maternal cousin, Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi'
She had two children "Ali and Umamah"

She died at the age of  30 (AH 7)and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.



Additional Information:


Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
1. Hazrat Qasim 
2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
6. Hazrat Abdullah
7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

314

Maqbool Hajj means_____?

Correct Answer: which is accepted
Show explanation
Explanation

There are three types of Hajj:

1.      Hajj-ul-Ifrad.

2.      Hajj-ul-Qiran.

3.      Hajj-ul-Tamattu.
315

Mabroor Hajj means______________?

Correct Answer: which is observed correctly
Show explanation
Explanation

There are three types of Hajj:

1.      Hajj-ul-Ifrad.

2.      Hajj-ul-Qiran.

3.      Hajj-ul-Tamattu.

316

Jannat al-Baqi is a ________?

Correct Answer: Cemetery
Show explanation
Explanation

Cemetery means قبرستان

Jannat al-Baqīʿ is the oldest and the first Islamic cemetery of Medina in the Hejazi region of present-day Saudi Arabia. It is located to the southeast of the Prophet's Mosque, which contains the graves of some of Prophet Muhammad's family and friends. It is also known as Baqīʿ al-Gharqad.

317

What is the literal meaning of Shariah?

Correct Answer: A clear straight path
Show explanation
Explanation

 Sharia literally means "the clear, well-trodden path to water".

Sharia acts as a code for living that all Muslims should adhere to, including prayers, fasting and donations to the poor.

318

Who are not entitled to get Zakat?

Correct Answer: Parents, Husband, wife and children
Show explanation
Explanation

The Eight Kinds of People Who Receive Zakat

1.           The poor (al-fuqarâ’)

2.           The needy (al-masâkîn)

3.           Zakat administrators.

4.           New Muslims and friends of the Muslim community.

5.           Slaves and captives.

6.           The debt-ridden.

7.           In the cause of God.

8.             The wayfarer, (travelling with few resources)

319

How many fasts Hazrat Isa (AS) Kept in desert and advised his followers to follow it?

Correct Answer: 40
Show explanation
Explanation

Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the desert to be tempted by the devil. After fasting for forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
320

Tawaf-e-Rukan is _____ component of Hajj?

Correct Answer: Farz
Show explanation
Explanation

Tawaf is circumambulating the Holy Ka’abah seven times, which starts and end on Hajr-e-Aswad in a counter clockwise direction
321

Hazrat Ishaaq (A.S) was the prophet of________________?

Correct Answer: Jews
Show explanation
Explanation

Isaac is one of the three patriarchs of the Israelites and is an important figure in the Abrahamic religions, including Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

He was the son of Abraham and Sarah, the father of Jacob, and the grandfather of the twelve tribes of Israel.

when the first Sariya was fought in Rabakh in_______________?

326

How many times has the Quran emphasized on alms or charity?

Correct Answer: 150 times
Show explanation
Explanation

قرآن مجید نے کتنی بار خیرات یا صدقات پر زور دیا ہے؟

150times

The commands to offer Salah are 700 times mentioned in the Quran.

The word Salah appears 67 times in the Quran.

328

___is the person who comes after one Rakat?

Correct Answer: Musbaq
Show explanation
Explanation

Munfarid is the person who performs prayer alone.
Madurak is the person who starts prayer with Immam
Musbaq is the person who comes after one rakat
329

The Fars News Agency is a news agency of:

Correct Answer: Iran
Show explanation
Explanation

News Agencies of Important Countries

Pakistan: Associated Press of Pakistan (APP)

China: Xinhua

Russia: TASS (Telegrafnoye agentstvo Sovetskogo Soyuza) 

India: Press Trust of India (PTI)

France: AFP (Agence France-Presse) (also oldest news agency)

Italy: ANSA (Agenzia Nazionale Stampa Associata)

USA: Associated Press (AP)

Britain: Reuters 

Malaysia: Bernama 

Israel: ITIM

Bahrain: BNA (Bahrain News Agency)

Iran: FARS

Syria: SANA(Syrian Arab News Agency)

Yemen: SABA

Indonesia: Antara 

Kuwait: KUNA (Kuwait News Agency)

Kenya: KNA (Kenya News Agency)


330

What are the sixth belief in Iman-e- Mufassal?

Correct Answer: Good and bad is predetermined by Allah
Show explanation
Explanation

Seven essential beliefs are there in Iman-e-Mafassal

1.  Faith in God.

2.  Faith in holy angels.

3.  Faith in all holy Books.

4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

7.  Faith in life after death.

331

What are the seventh belief in Iman-e- Mufassal?

Correct Answer: Day of Resurrection
Show explanation
Explanation

Seven essential beliefs are there in Iman-e-Mafassal

1.  Faith in God.

2.  Faith in holy angels.

3.  Faith in all holy Books.

4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

7.  Faith in life after death.

332

The function of Hazrat Mea(keAS) is to______________?

Correct Answer: Make rain and supply of food to Allah’s creatures
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Jibrael (علیہ) delivered the Message to every Prophet from Hazrat Adam to Muhammad (pbuh).

Izra'il – The Angel of Death, who takes the souls from bodies when people die.

Israfil – The angel who will be present on the day of resurrection .

Mīkāl_ is often depicted as providing nourishment for bodies and souls while also being responsible for bringing rain and thunder to Earth.

333

What is the punishment of drinking wines in Quran?

Correct Answer: None of these
Show explanation
Explanation
The majority of scholars are of the view that it is eighty lashes for a free man and forty for others.
As per the Quran, there are no punishments mentioned.

334

Which is the national bird of China?

Correct Answer: Red-Crowned Crane
Show explanation
Explanation

 China Capital is Beijing.

China Currency is Chinese Yuan.

China official language is Mandarin.

China located is in Continent Asia.

China Primary Religion is N/A.

China Head of Govt. is President - Xi Jinping.

China Parliament called National People’s Assembly.

Table tennis is the national game of China.

 Red-Crowned Crane is the national bird of China.





 

335

Which bird is mentioned in Holy Quran ?

Correct Answer: Hudhud
Show explanation
Explanation

The bird appears twice in the 27th chapter of An-Naml.

Hudhud, as described by the Quran played an important role between Sulayman and Queen of Sheba while carrying messages between the two.

336

What was the name of first woman Muslim?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Khdija
Show explanation
Explanation

  • Hazrat Khadija belonged to the Quraish tribe. She is also known as “Khatijatul Kubra”
  • Prophert ﷺ married her when he was 25 years old and she was 40 years old. .
  • She passed away at the age of 65 years on the 11th of Ramadaan – 10 years after the first revelation.

338

Hazart Ali (R.A) married Hazart Fatma (R.A) in______________?

Correct Answer: 624 A.D
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazart Fatma (رضہ) marriage took place on Friday, 1 Dhu al-Hijjah AH 2 (24 February AD 624) when Fatimah was nine years old, although some sources say she was 18 years old. Hazrat Ali was 21

339

The Sahabi-e-Rasool and cousin of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) is known as Molood-e-Kaaba Sharif is?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri,

before the time of FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.

He died on the 21st of Ramadan 40 A.H or  28 January 661 CE.

He was buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

Hazrat Ali (R.A): Molood-e-Kaaba Sharif 




340

How many types of Wahi are there in Islam ____?

Correct Answer: 2
Show explanation
Explanation

There are two types of Wahi in Islam.

1. Wahee-e-Matlu

2. Wahi-e-Ghair Matlu

341

کونسی مسجد کو گرانے کا حکم دیا کیا؟

Correct Answer: مسجد ضرار
Show explanation
Explanation
342

Which pillar of Islam extinguishes sin, just as water extinguishes fire?

Correct Answer: Charity (Sadaqah)
Show explanation
Explanation

“Charity (Sadaqah) extinguishes sin, just as water extinguishes fire.”


―Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (Tirmidhi 614)



“Verily, the good deeds remove the evil deeds” ―Quran 11:114


Charity (ZAKAH) is the third pillar of Islam. 

343

The red sea is connected to which Gulf or sea through Bab al-Mandeb Strait?

Correct Answer: Gulf of Aden
Show explanation
Explanation

The Bab-el-Mandeb 'Gate of Lamentation' or the Gate of Grief

the Bab al-Mandab strait is a strait between Yemen on the Arabian Peninsula and Djibouti and Eritrea in the Horn of Africa.

connecting the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden and the Indian Ocean.

The Bab-el-Mandeb is also a sub-region in the Arab League, which includes Djibouti, Yemen, and Eritrea.

ND16-3-2023

344

The function of Hazrat jibra’eel (AS) is to______________?

Correct Answer: Bring Allah’s message and commands to his prophets (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Jibrael (علیہ) delivered the Message to every Prophet from Hazrat Adam to Muhammad (pbuh).

Izra'il – The Angel of Death, who takes the souls from bodies when people die.

Israfil – The angel who will be present on the day of resurrection .

Mīkāl_ is often depicted as providing nourishment for bodies and souls while also being responsible for bringing rain and thunder to Earth.

345

Two angels are appointed on each person. One of them maintains the good deeds and the other one records evil deeds. These two angels are named as?

Correct Answer: Kiraman katibeen (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

In Islamic tradition the two kiraman katibin are two angels called Raqib and Atid, believed by Muslims to record a person's actions.

346

The first Kalima consists of how many dots _____?

Correct Answer: Zero
Show explanation
Explanation


347

The father's name of Hazrat Ali (R.A) was?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Talib (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The age of hazrat Ali (A.S) was 61
  • **
  • At age 10, ʿAlī accepted Islam,
  • Hazrat Ali die: at 61 years (599 AD–661 AD)
  • Hazrat Ali, the cousin and son-in-law of Prophet Muhammad is believed to have been the first male to adopt Islam
  • **
  • Ali was killed in the mosque of Kufa by the sword of one of the Kharijites, Ibn Muljam Moradi.
  • Buried outside the city of Kufa.
  • His shrine and the city of Najaf were built around his tomb

348

My aunt has invited me to her place _____ three o’clock this afternoon _____ tea.

Correct Answer: At, for
Show explanation
Explanation

We use preposition at before clock. Like at 7 o' clock, at 9 o'clock, at 4 o' clock.

We use preposition for to tell a reason. (Here tea is the reason of invitation) 

My aunt has invited me to her place at three o’clock this afternoon for tea.

349

There are ________ schools of thought in Islamic fiqh.

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

the terminological meaning of 'Fiqh is "deep understanding"

Fiqh refers to the body of Islamic law extracted from detailed Islamic sources 


There are the following Five Schools of Islamic Thought.

  1. Hanafi
  2. Maliki
  3. Shafi΄i
  4. Hanbali

ND19-3-2023

350

Allah said this worship is mine and I will reward the virtues

Correct Answer: Fasting
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Bukhaari (1761) and Muslim (1946) narrated that Abu Hurayrah (may Allaah be pleased with him) said: The Messenger of Allaah (peace and blessings of Allaah be upon him) said: “Allaah said: ‘Every deed of the son of Adam is for him except fasting; it is for Me and I shall reward for it…’” 


351

al Furqan al Huda al Kitab al Hakim are the other name of Quran found in:

Correct Answer: quran
Show explanation
Explanation

al Furqan al Huda al Kitab al Hakim are the other name of Quran found in quran:

Allah (SWT) mentioned many attributes of Quran in Quran.

It is said that there are about 50 different names that illustrates the role of Quran in our life.

  1. Al Quran Which is read 2:185
  2. Al Kitab The Book 2:2
  3. Al Furqan The Criterion 25:1
  4. Al Zikr The Reminder 21:50
  5. Al Mubeen The Clear 43:2
  6. Al Kareem The Noble, Bounteous 56:77
  7. Kalamallah The Words of Allah 2:75
  8. Al Noor The Light 4:174
  9. Al Maw?izah Exhortation 3:138
  10. Al A?aliyyun High 43:4
  11. Al Rahmat The Mercy 17:82
  12. Al Shifa The Healing 17:82
  13. Al Mubarak The Blessed 6:155
  14. Al Bayan The Declaration 3:138
  15. Al Ilm The Knowledge 3:61
  16. Al Haqq The Truth 17:81
  17. Al Huda The Guidance 17:9
  18. Al ?Ajab The Wonderful 72:1
  19. Al Tazkira The Admonition 73:19
  20. Al urwatil uthka The most trustworthy handhold 31:22
  21. Al Sidq The Truth 39:33
  22. Al Hikmah The Effective Wisdom 54:5
  23. Al Adl The Just 6:115
  24. Al Amrullah The Command of Allah 65:5
  25. Al Munadi The Caller 3:193
  26. Al Bushra The Glad Tidings 2:97
  27. Al Majeed The Glorious 85:21
  28. Al Nazir The Warner 41:4
  29. Al Azeez The Invincible 41:41
  30. Al Balag The Message 14:52
  31. Al Suhufim Mukarammah Books of great Honor 80:13
  32. Al Marfua  The High  80:14
  33. Al Musaddiq The Testimony 2:89
  34. Al Burhan  The Proof 4:174
  35. Al Hakeem  The wise, Full of wisdom 10:1
  36. Al Muhaymin  The guardian 5:48
  37. Habl Allah  The rope of Allah 3:103
  38. Al Fasl  The conclusive 86:13
  39. Ahsan al Hadeeth  The most beautiful message 39:23
  40. Al Qayiim  The straight 98:3
  41. Al Mathani  The consistent 39:23
  42. Al Mutashabih  The Allegoric 39:23
  43. Al Tanzeel The  Revealed 56:80
  44. Al Rooh  The Spirit 42:52
  45. Al Wahy  The Inspiration 21:45
  46. Haqqul Yaqin  Truth with Certainty 56:95
  47. Al Arabi  The Arabic 12:2
352

Who wrote Al Fiqh Al Islami wa Adillatahu?

Correct Answer: Wahba Al-Zuhayli
Show explanation
Explanation

Wahba Al-Zuhayli wrote Al Fiqh Al Islami wa Adillatahu.

**

al-Fiqh al-Islāmī wa adillatuhu written by Wahbah Zuḥaylī

 Publish Date of al-Fiqh al-Islāmī wa adillatuhu. 2003.

 Publisher of al-Fiqh al-Islāmī wa adillatuhu: Dār al-Fikr al-Mouaser

ND19-3-2023

353

At the time of Hijrat e Madina who was with Muhammad PBUH?

Correct Answer: Abu Bakar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • At the time of Hijrat e Madina, Abu Bakar was with Muhammad PBUH
  • He is the father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad
  • First Caliph of the Rashidun Caliphate.

Additional information

  • After Hijrat from Mecca to Madina, the Holy Prophet Muhammad’s (PBUH) camel kneeled at the door of Hazrat Ayub Ansari
  • At the Hijrat e Madina, which Surah is recited by Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), Surah e Yaseen
  • Abdullah Bin Ariqat (R.A) was appointed as Usher for Hijrat E Madina






354

The Meaning of Kauthar (Surah Kauser ) is

Correct Answer: The Abundance
Show explanation
Explanation

The Meaning of Kauthar is The Abundance.

The Al-Kawthar (الكوثر) is the Shortest Surah of holy Quran.

it has only 3 verses.

355

When the permission for Tayammum was granted?

Correct Answer: Battle of Rajih.4hij
Show explanation
Explanation

Tayammum is the Islamic act of dry Ritual purification using purified sand or dust

in Al-Ma'idah contains the order of wuzu, Ghusal, and Tayammum

Tayammum is valid only for one obligatory Salah

Tayammum is obligatory on 4 (AH)

while 

Wazu is obligatory on 5(AH)

****

356

Hazrat Qasim was the son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and _______.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Qasim was the first (elder) son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

He died at the age of only 3 and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


Additional Information:

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

Hazrat Khadija (RA) was the first wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ

At the time of the marriage, Hazrat Khadijah was 40 years old. Muhammad was 25 years old.

357

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) started to preach openly in ……Nabvi.

Correct Answer: 4th Nabvi
Show explanation
Explanation

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) started to preach openly in the 4th Nabvi

***

The Holy Prophet (PBUH) first openly preached Islam on Jabil-e-Safa and addressed it after the conquest of Makkah in the same place.

23 years did Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ preach Islam during his lifetime

Hazrat Musab ibn Umair (RA was the first preacher of Islam appointed by Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ for the people of Madina

358

Muslim strength in battle of Uhud?

Correct Answer: 1000
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.

      Abu Sufyan commander of the infidels in  Uhad Ghazwa

      ***

      It was fought in 3 Hijri 

      Ohad is the name of a mountain

        1000 Muslims participated in the battle of Uhad

        3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad Infidel means کفار

          In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected 50Skillful archers to stay on the mountain

          The Qurayshi Meccans, led by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb,

          Toward Muhammad's stronghold in Medina.

          359

          During migration to Madina Prophet (SAW) riding on which animal?

          Correct Answer: Camel
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          ===

          The Prophet Hazrat Muhammad's have three she-camels:

          1. Qaswa
          2. Al-Jad`aa,
          3. Al-Adbaa’.

          364

          What are the Forth belief in Iman-e- Mufassal?

          Correct Answer: The prophets
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Seven essential beliefs are there in Iman-e-Mafassal

          1.  Faith in God.

          2.  Faith in holy angels.

          3.  Faith in all holy Books.

          4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

          5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

          6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

          7.  Faith in life after death.

          365

          Maghrib prayer is offered just after the ____

          Correct Answer: Sun set
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Namaz commanded in Quran for 700 times.

          Salat made compulsory in 10th Hijri.

          Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.

          366

          Who is known as first mart yard of Karbala ?

          Correct Answer: Hurr Ibn Yazid
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          When did Karbala take place?
          The Battle of Karbala took place on           Muharram 10, in the year 61 of the Islamic calendar (October 10, 680) in Karbala, in present day Iraq. On one side were supporters and relatives of Muhammad's grandson Husain ibn Ali, on the other side was a military detachment from the forces of Yazid I, the Umayyad caliph.
          367

          Which Umm ul Momineen died first?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Khadija (رضہ)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Khadeja (رضہ) was first female who accepted islam in women.

          Died in 10th Nabvi
          368

          How many years was the entire Quran revealed

          Correct Answer: 23
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Quran has 30 Paras

          114 surah

          6666 ayat


          369

          Where is the grave of Hazrat Loot (علیہ)?

          Correct Answer: Iraq
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Loot was born to Hazrat Haran.
          Prophet Loot AS was the nephew of the           Prophet Ibrahim AS.
          370

          Who was called as kidnapper of the scholars?

          Correct Answer: Mahmood Ghaznavi
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Muhammad Habib, author of the book Mehmud of Ghazni, called him the kidnapper of poets and historians because whenever he heard about a famous scholar, he ordered the rulers, under whose services they were to send them to his court.
          371

          Apollo___ placed the copy of Quran on the moon

          Correct Answer: 15
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Apollo 15  was the ninth crewed mission in the United States' Apollo program and the fourth to land on the Moon.

          372

          Zaboor is revealed in __ language

          Correct Answer: Siriac
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Holy book Torat was revealed on Hazrat Moosa (A.S)

          The Holy book Anjeel was revealed on Hazrat Isa (A.S)

          The Holy book Zaboor was revealed on Hazrat Dawood (Siriac language)

          The Holy book Quran was revealed on Hazrat Muhammad (S.A.W).(Arabic language)

          373

          The meaning of Muharram is ?

          Correct Answer: Forbidden
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          Muharram means "forbidden" since it is considered to be holy.
          Muharram also marks the anniversary of the battle of Karbala.
          where the Islamic prophet Muhammad's grandson Imam Hussain Ibn Ali was killed
          Muḥarram is the first month of the Islamic calendar.
          The tenth day of Muharram is known as Ashura
          Number of days: 29-30 (depends on actual observation of the moon's crescent)
          374

          What is meant by Tawaaf?

          Correct Answer: To circle around Kaaba
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Tawaf al-Qudum knewhaj as"The Tawaf of Arrival" is the initial Tawaf carried out upon entering Masjid al-Haram in Makkah.

          375

          Who Was the head of that Board, which was established for the preparation of copies of Musaef Abi Bakar?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          During the 23 years of Muhammad's time as a prophet, the verses of the Quran were memorized as they were revealed, and about 42 scribes wrote the verses on different materials such as paper, cloth, bone fragments and leather.

          The Quran collected between two binders by Abu Bakr was transferred to Umar after he died.

          After Umar died, it was transferred to his daughter Hazrat Hifsa

          376

          How many ayat e Mutafarq are present in Holy Quran?

          Correct Answer: 66
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The number of verses in the Quran is 6,666.

          In fact, the total number of verses in Quraan is 6,236 excluding Bismillah and 6349 including Bismillah.

          There are 114 Surah in the Qur'an.

          Juz 30

          Mnazil 7

          14 Sajda talawat

          377

          What is the first manifestation of faith in Islam?

          Correct Answer: Shahadah
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Shahadah, profession of faith, is the first pillar of Islam.
          • Muslims bear witness to the oneness of God by reciting the creed “There is no God but God and Muhammad is the Messenger of God.”
          • This simple yet profound statement expresses a Muslim’s complete acceptance of and total commitment to Islam.
          378

          The action of Islam depends on

          Correct Answer: Faith
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Iman in Islamic theology denotes a believer's faith in the metaphysical aspects of Islam.

          Its most simple definition is the belief in the six articles of faith, known as arkān al-īmān.

          The term iman has been delineated in both the Quran and hadith.

          379

          The prerequisite to be a Muslim is

          Correct Answer: both
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Shahadah,is the first pillar of Islam.

          Muslims bear witness to the oneness of God by reciting the creed "There is no God but God and Muhammad is the Messenger of God."

          380

          4 or 8 or 12 rakat offered in the late night is called ?

          Correct Answer: Salat e Tahajjud
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Tahajjud, also known as the "night prayer", is a voluntary prayer performed by followers of Islam.

          It is not one of the five obligatory prayers required of all Muslims

          Nafl Prayer

          381

          Muslim perform Hajj on ---- of Zulhaj.

          Correct Answer: 9th day
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The rites of pilgrimage are performed over five to six days, extending from the 8th to the 12th or 13th of Dhu al-Hijjah.

          382

          From where pabels for throwing on Jumerat are obtained ?

          Correct Answer: Muzdalfa
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Seven stones are thrown at each of the three pillars to commemorate Ibrahim's rejection of Satan.

          383

          After __ days continuous rain and storm the nation of Hood destroyed

          Correct Answer: 7
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hud was a prophet of ancient Arabia mentioned in the Qur’an.

          The eleventh chapter of the Quran, Hud or Hood.


          384

          To perform Salat e Istikhara is?

          Correct Answer: Nafal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Salat-al-Istikhara is a prayer of guidance recited during times of indecision.

          To perform Istikhara, you should first be cleansed, so perform Wudu if necessary. 

          385

          JALSA in Namaz is ____?

          Correct Answer: Wajib
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          A Rakat is one complete cycle of:

          Standing (Qa'adah),

          Bending (Rukoo),

          Prostrations (Sajdah)

          subsequent Sitting (jalsa) 
          386

          On which of following Zakat money can be spent?

          Correct Answer: Poor Muslims
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          With the literal meaning of the word being 'to cleanse,' Muslims believe that paying zakat purifies, increases and blesses the remainder of their wealth.

          zakat is a religious duty for all Muslims who meet the necessary criteria of wealth. 

          Zakat literally means Purification.

          When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H.

          The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

          Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat


          387

          How many units (rakat) taraveh are offered in Ramadan?

          Correct Answer: 20
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Total 20 Rakat Taraweeh offered in Ramadan.

          Ramazan is 9th month of Islamic calander
          388

          How many stones are thrown at the Jamarat in Hajj ritual?

          Correct Answer: 7 Stones
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Seven stones are thrown at each of the three pillars to commemorate Ibrahim's rejection of Satan.
          389

          In Islam legislation refers to---?

          Correct Answer: Rules and regulation for governing a nation
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          ‘Islamic law’ refers to the diverse legal systems that have been and continue to be produced with the objective of being in accord with the Islamic faith.

          390

          Give the fundamental beliefs of Islam in the proper order?

          Correct Answer: Tauheed, Risalat, Malaika, Alhami Kutub and Akhirat,
          Show explanation
          Explanation


          391

          According to Islam all praises are for

          Correct Answer: Allah Almighty
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          "all praise is due to God alone" (Muhammad Asad) "all the praises and thanks be to God"

          392

          The distance between Makkah and Medina is about:

          Correct Answer: 450 km
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Distance between Mecca and Medina in km is around 450 km but the shortest distance which is known as displacement between is 342 km= 213milesThe conquest of Makka in December 629 or January 630 

          The nickname of Makka Bakkah

          The nickname of Madine Yasrib

          393

          How many times Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ) is mentioned in Sura Baqarah?

          Correct Answer: 13
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          AL-BAQARAH (the Cow) has been so named from the story of the Cow occurring in this Surah (vv. 67-73).

          Al-Baqarah is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.

          It consists of 286 verses

          282 verse in chapter Al-Baqara. this verse is the longest verse in the longest chapter of the Quran

          The 282 verse of Surah Al Baqara is known as 'āyatu d-dayn) 

          Position: Juzʼ 1–3

          Classification: Medinan

          No. of Rukus: 40

           25,500 letters

          13 Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ) is mentioned in Sura Baqarah

          183 Ayat of Surah Al-Baqarah describes the obligation of soam


          ND16-3-2023

          394

          Which Ayat of Surah Al-Baqarah describes the obligation of soam?

          Correct Answer: 183
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          AL-BAQARAH (the Cow) has been so named from the story of the Cow occurring in this Surah (vv. 67-73).

          Al-Baqarah is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.

          It consists of 286 verses

          282 verse in chapter Al-Baqara. this verse is the longest verse in the longest chapter of the Quran

          The 282 verse of Surah Al Baqara is known as 'āyatu d-dayn) 

          Position: Juzʼ 1–3

          Classification: Medinan

          No. of Rukus: 40

           25,500 letters
